questions.text: After-hours badge swipes in a data center from a terminated contractor badge. Action?

options.text: Deactivate badge, review CCTV, and audit access logs for tailgating partners
Ignore if door locked, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable fire alarms, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Share badge with security guard, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Physical access logs must tie to active authorization.
questions.concept: Tailgating and propped doors defeat physical controls—train and monitor.

questions.text: A propped-open fire door alarm is muted for 'convenience.' Risk?

options.text: Required for HIPAA Wi-Fi, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Improves cooling only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Stops phishing, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Unauthorized entry and fire code violation

questions.explanation: Prop alarms exist to prevent uncontrolled entry.
questions.concept: Tailgating and propped doors defeat physical controls—train and monitor.

questions.text: Visitor logs are blank but deliveries increased. Control gap?

options.text: Over-patching servers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Visitor procedures document who entered sensitive areas.

questions.text: Tailgating incidents rise after badge readers fail open during a fire drill. Mitigation?

options.text: Remove badges and use honor-system sign-in sheets at the front desk, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Prop doors during business hours to reduce reader wear and tear, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable fire alarms to prevent future fail-open events on the access system
Repair readers, reinforce training, and add reception or camera verification during peaks

questions.explanation: Fail-open events increase tailgating; combine physical controls with awareness.
questions.concept: Tailgating and propped doors defeat physical controls—train and monitor.

questions.text: Laptop stolen from a car; drive encrypted, device not managed. Concern?

options.text: Encryption useless, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
If logged in recently, data in use may be accessible; no remote wipe
Theft impossible on Mac, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
HIPAA bans laptops, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.

questions.explanation: MDM enables wipe and attestation; encryption helps at rest only.
questions.concept: Encryption and remote wipe reduce impact of lost laptops and mobile devices.

questions.text: Spare phones disappear from a stock room without inventory updates. Process fix?

options.text: Disable all mobile MDM, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Asset tracking with check-in/out and alarm on high-value storage
Leave room unlocked for convenience, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Publish IMEI list publicly, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Inventory controls deter insider theft and aid recovery.
questions.concept: Encryption and remote wipe reduce impact of lost laptops and mobile devices.

questions.text: Stolen tablet had clinical app logged in. Immediate steps?

options.text: Remote wipe via MDM, force session logout, breach assessment per HIPAA
Post patient list online, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Wait for user to buy replacement, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable encryption, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.

questions.explanation: Mobile clinical theft requires wipe and PHI breach evaluation.
questions.concept: Encryption and remote wipe reduce impact of lost laptops and mobile devices.

questions.text: An employee's encrypted laptop is stolen from a car; BitLocker was on but the session unlocked. Risk?

options.text: Fixed automatically when the user changes their desktop wallpaper, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Risk only if the laptop was not registered in the asset inventory spreadsheet, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Data at rest is protected; data in use may be accessible until credentials are rotated
No risk because theft of encrypted devices never leads to data disclosure, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Encryption helps at rest; unlocked sessions may still expose data and tokens.
questions.concept: Encryption and remote wipe reduce impact of lost laptops and mobile devices.

questions.text: Employees lend badges to coworkers. Policy fix?

options.text: Disable access logs, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Remove cameras, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Layered physical entry controls reduce tailgating.

questions.text: NVR firmware is three years old on the security VLAN. Recommendation?

options.text: Share admin password with vendors via email, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Expose NVR to internet for remote viewing, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

Disable authentication, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.

Privacy impact, consent, retention limits, and bias risks require legal/privacy review
